sourceEvidence.label: Pilon Fracture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Pilon Fracture

objectives: Recognize high-energy tibial plafond fractures with soft-tissue risk.
Explain staged management with external fixation bridging.
Identify wound complications and articular restoration priorities.

sourceEvidence.label: Pilon Fracture — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Pilon Fracture

mustKnow: Soft tissue status, articular comminution, staged fixation, fibula role
payload.oneLiner: Pilon fractures are ankle-spanning injuries where soft tissues often dictate staged operative timing.
pimpQuestions: When is fibula fixation part of the plan?

sourceEvidence.label: Pilon Fracture — Deep study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Deep study template for Pilon Fracture

decisionMaking: Soft-tissue swelling, open injury, articular displacement
boardPearls: Definitive fixation through compromised soft tissues increases wound failure risk

metadata.description: Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.
Four-compartment osteofascial model shared with compartment syndrome neighborhood.

relationship_metadata.note: do not rush definitive fixation through compromised skin

metadata.claim_text: Planning pilon ORIF from radiographs alone without CT articular mapping of comminution and step-off.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.

relationship_metadata.note: distal tibial plafond localized via tibial-shaft shared slug

metadata.description: Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.

metadata.claim_text: Fibular length and lateral column restoration support ankle alignment during pilon reconstruction.
Pilon fractures are intra-articular distal tibial injuries where soft tissues often dictate staged reconstruction.
Do not rush definitive pilon fixation through compromised skin — staging protects against wound catastrophe.

metadata.description: Spanning external fixation and soft-tissue recovery before definitive plafond reconstruction.

metadata.claim_text: Document soft-tissue status, articular injury pattern, fibula role, staging plan, and definitive fixation timing.

metadata.action: Apply staged pilon protocol with spanning external fixation and delayed definitive ORIF when skin allows
metadata.trigger: Pilon fracture with severe swelling, fracture blisters, or soft-tissue compromise

metadata.action: Proceed with definitive pilon ORIF including fibular length restoration and articular reduction
metadata.trigger: Soft tissues recovered with unacceptable articular step-off or instability on CT

metadata.description: Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Anterior leg compartment at risk after tibial and pilon injuries.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.

metadata.description: Severe swelling, fracture blisters, or skin compromise delaying definitive fixation.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
High-energy intra-articular distal tibial plafond fracture with soft-tissue compromise risk.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Wound breakdown or infection after pilon fracture operative management.
Lateral leg bone influencing alignment and length in leg injuries.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Definitive open reduction internal fixation of tibial plafond articular injury.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Subcutaneous tibial diaphysis with limited soft-tissue envelope.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.
